title: Rollout Plan
description: How a groomed feature actually reaches production without breaking the book. Every feature carries a mechanism, staged gates with guardrail metrics, and a rollback path — the same discipline the trading side applies to capital, applied to shipping.

mechanism.description: How the change is introduced and controlled. new_module = additive, off unless invoked; config_knob/feature_flag = default-off toggle; ci_gate = enforced in CI.

flag.description: The concrete switch name (config attr, env var, CLI flag) or 'n/a' for a pure ci_gate. Default-off is the rule unless justified in notes.

default_on.description: Whether the feature is on by default at merge. Defaults to false; a true value should be justified by a passed prior stage.

stages.description: Ordered gates from first-merge to full-on. Each stage must state what is validated before advancing.

scope.description: What/where it runs at this stage, e.g. 'US sleeve only, dry_run'.

exit_criteria.description: The measurable condition to advance to the next stage.

guardrail_metrics.description: Metrics that must NOT regress during rollout. If any breaches, the rollback triggers.

threshold.description: e.g. 'turnover within +/-10% of baseline', 'no new lookahead test failures'.

rollback.description: The exact action to revert safely, e.g. 'set config.DATA_QUALITY_GATE=False; prior behaviour restored, no state migration needed'.
